requiredFiles.reason: Make export requests reuse the incident's oldest open job while preserving status lookup and missing-incident handling.
Implement per-incident lookup of the oldest queued or processing job and retain creation ordering.
Resume the oldest queued or processing job and build a strictly allowlisted export bundle.
Make the generated test directory an independently importable test package.
Add standalone unittest coverage for deduplication, ordering, resumption, status lookup, and sanitization.

acceptanceCriteria: Repeated export requests for one incident return that incident's oldest job whose status is queued or processing.
Completed jobs are not reused; a new job is created when an incident has no open job.
Requesting an export for an unknown incident raises KeyError.
The worker processes the oldest job across both queued and processing statuses and returns None when no open job exists.
A processed job is saved with status completed.
The completed bundle contains exactly id, title, status, severity, owner, customer_summary, and timeline.
Every timeline entry contains exactly at and message.
The bundle never exports internal_notes, api_token, or internal actor identity fields.
Status lookup continues returning the current persisted job by job ID.
Clicking #watchIncident still sets watching to "true" and displays "Watching incident".
Incident save/get behavior and title formatting such as "INC-7: Checkout latency" remain unchanged.
index.html continues referencing dashboard.js and the detailsPanel element.
implementationChecklist: Implement find_open_escalation_job to filter by incident ID and queued/processing status and return the first job in creation order.
Update request_escalation_export to return the existing oldest open job before creating a new one.
Keep get_escalation_status delegated to repository lookup.
Select the worker's oldest queued or processing job without prioritizing one open status over the other.
Persist queued work as processing before final completion so processing jobs are resumable.
Construct bundles with explicit top-level and timeline allowlists rather than copying the incident dictionary.
Save the sanitized bundle and completed status through the repository.
Add unittest cases with reset repository state and an executable unittest.main entry point.
verificationChecklist: Verify duplicate requests reuse an older queued job and an older processing job.
Verify a completed older job is ignored and a new job is created when no open job remains.
Verify requests for two incident IDs remain distinct.
Verify the worker chooses an older processing job over a newer queued job and an older queued job over a newer processing job.
Verify completed jobs are skipped and no open work returns None.
Assert exact bundle key sets and exact timeline-entry key sets using incidents containing forbidden and unrelated fields.
Verify internal_notes, api_token, actor, actor_id, and other internal identity metadata do not appear in the bundle.
Verify API status lookup reflects queued and completed states and unknown job lookup remains non-breaking.
Run generated tests independently without network listeners or external calls.
Run the immutable baseline suite to catch existing dashboard, storage, formatting, and markup regressions.
